figures.caption: Figure 1: Illustration of harmonic inpainting with a 5% mask. Inpainting MSE: 38.24.
Figure 2: Illustration of EED inpainting with a 5% mask. Inpainting MSE: 14.95.

Figure 3: Harmonic inpainting using only gray-value features versus five features (gray values, ∂x, ∂y, 3× 3 and 5× 5 binomial kernels). The mask density is 5% in both cases. The reconstruction error is more than halved despite using the same inpainting operator and mask density. Note the significantly improved texture reconstruction of the hat.

Figure 4: Illustration of EED inpainting using only gray-value features versus five features (gray values, ∂x, ∂y, 3× 3 and 5× 5 binomial kernels). The total mask density is 5% in both cases. The reconstruction error improves by nearly 28% compared to the already strong performance of classical EED inpainting.
Figure 5: A visualization of the Voronoi tessellation induced by a mask, a piecewise-constant approximation, and the original image.
Table 1: MSEs for harmonic inpainting with an increasing number of feature families.

Figure 6: Harmonic inpainting with linear features, 5% mask. Note the large improvements in the closing of the edges and the suppression of the logarithmic singularities inherent to harmonic inpainting.
Table 2: MSEs for EED inpainting with an increasing number of feature families.
Figure 7: EED inpainting with linear features, 5% mask. Note the much sharper result and better reconstructed edges.
Figure 8: Checkered texture reconstructed with 30 densification iterations at 40% density: pointwise (left) vs pointwise and the nonlinear affine-invariant curvature features (middle).
Figure 9: Harmonic inpainting. Left: color values only; middle: all five features; right: original. Photos by Joachim Weickert – top to bottom: raindeer, quai, mirror, crab.
Figure 10: EED inpainting. Left: color values only; middle: all five feature types; right: original. Photos by Joachim Weickert – top to bottom: boats, madeira, garafia, flowers.
Figure 11: Harmonic inpainting results for a combined density of 3% for elpaso produced with 30 densification iterations.
Figure 12: Harmonic inpainting results for a combined density of 5% for windmill produced with 30 densification iterations.
Figure 13: EED inpainting results for a combined density of 3% for elpaso produced with 30 densification iterations
Figure 14: EED inpainting results for a combined density of 5% for windmill produced with 30 densification iterations.
